channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Understanding Grooming Tactics: Lessons from the Epstein Case
item.description: In this Full Story podcast episode, journalist Lucia Osborne-Crowley, author of 'The Lasting Harm,' discusses the grooming tactics employed by Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell. Osborne-Crowley em...

item.title: Senator Ibrahim Praise Tinubu’s UK Visit as Strategic Diplomatic Move
item.description: Senator Jimoh Ibrahim, wey be chairman of the Senate Committee on Inter-Parliamentary Affairs and represent Ondo South, don hail President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for him recent diplomatic visit to the Unit...

item.title: Air Canada Express Flight Crashes into Fire Truck at LaGuardia Airport, Two Pilots Killed
item.description: An Air Canada Express flight operated by Jazz Aviation crashed into a fire truck on the runway at LaGuardia Airport in New York late on Sunday night. The flight, which departed from Montreal, was a CR...

item.title: A Glimpse into African-Inspired Poetry and the Journey of Decolonising Thought
item.description: The article presents an excerpt from ‘The Alphabets of Africa’ by Abhay K, exploring the profound experiences and reflections of African writers. Abdulrazak Gurnah shares his struggle with homesicknes...

item.title: Thousands Participate in Kilimanjaro International Marathon Amid Rain in Moshi
item.description: Despite heavy rains in Kilimanjaro Region, the 2026 Kilimanjaro International Marathon saw thousands of participants and spectators in Moshi braving the wet weather. Kenyan runners dominated the 42km ...

item.title: Leo Radvinsky, OnlyFans owner, dies aged 43 following cancer battle
item.description: Leo Radvinsky, the billionaire owner of the adult content platform OnlyFans, has died at the age of 43 after a prolonged battle with cancer. According to a company spokesperson, Radvinsky passed away ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Discusses Regional Peace with Iranian President Pezeshkian
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif had a telephonic conversation with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on March 23, 2026. During the call, PM Shehbaz extended greetings for both Eid ul Fitr and Nowruz t...

item.title: Australians Increasingly Worried About National Security, Survey Shows
item.description: Australians are becoming increasingly concerned about national security, according to a recent survey conducted by the Australian National University’s National Security College. Over 20,000 Australia...

item.title: Ontario Legislature Returns: Ford Government Faces Scrutiny Over Transparency and Student Aid Cuts
item.description: The Ontario legislature is set to resume sitting on Monday, March 23, 2026, after an unusually long winter break called by the Progressive Conservative government. This resumption marks the first sess...

item.title: Philippine peso weakens further amid continued dollar strength and market uncertainty
item.description: The Philippine peso slipped to another record low against the US dollar on Monday, continuing a downward trend that has persisted since early this year. The local currency breached a new low as trader...

item.title: Middle East Conflict's Impact on Sub-Saharan Africa's Financing Sources
item.description: Sub-Saharan African countries rely on external funding like foreign direct investment, migrant remittances, aid, loans, and internal domestic markets, all now under threat, worsened by the Middle East...

item.title: CIBD warns construction mafia still poses a risk despite recent progress
item.description: The Construction Industry Development Board (CIBD) has issued a warning that while the construction mafia in South Africa has been significantly weakened, it has not been entirely eradicated. CIBD CEO...

item.title: Four Jewish Volunteer Ambulances Destroyed in Suspected Antisemitic Arson in London
item.description: Several ambulances belonging to the Jewish volunteer rescue organization Hatzola Northwest were deliberately set on fire outside the Machzikei Hadath synagogue in Golders Green, north London, early Mo...

item.title: Cursor's Composer 2 Model Uses Moonshot AI’s Kimi as a Base, Enhanced with Additional Training
item.description: Cursor, a U.S.-based AI coding company, recently launched Composer 2, a model touted as offering advanced coding capabilities. Shortly after the release, an X user highlighted that Composer 2 was larg...

item.title: Understanding the Role of Asymptomatic TB in South Africa's Epidemic
item.description: Tuberculosis (TB) remains a major health challenge in South Africa, claiming over 50 000 lives annually despite being curable. One of the factors complicating TB control is asymptomatic TB, where indi...

item.title: Government Identifies Potential Sites for New Towns Across England
item.description: The government has unveiled plans to develop 'new towns' across England, narrowing the list of potential sites to seven for further consideration. This initiative, first announced at the Labour party ...

item.title: Former Kirinyaga Senator Daniel Karaba Dies After Long Career in Education and Politics
item.description: Former Kirinyaga Senator Daniel Karaba has passed away at the age of 78, with the news confirmed by Kirinyaga Governor Anne Waiguru on Monday. In her statement, Waiguru described Karaba as a humble an...

item.title: A Gaza Teen’s Disappearance and the Mass Crisis of the Missing in Gaza
item.description: The article follows the story of 16‑year‑old Hassan, an autistic teenager from Gaza, who vanished in 2024 after riding his bicycle from his family’s home during the war. His parents, Abeer and Ali, ha...

item.title: Antisemitic arson attack targets Jewish community ambulances in London
item.description: Four ambulances belonging to a Jewish community organisation, Hatzola, were set on fire in north London during the night in what British Prime Minister Keir Starmer described as a deeply disturbing ac...

item.title: Modi Assures Economic Stability Amid Rupee Decline and Energy Crisis
item.description: In the wake of the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran, India’s rupee has dropped to an all-time low of 93.84 against the US dollar, raising concerns over energy supply disruptions and rising costs. Despite...
